revision.comment: [[奈良時代]]の[[貴族]]
revision.text:
'''上毛野 広人'''（かみつけの の ひろひと、生年不詳 - [[養老]]4年（[[720年]]）9月）は、[[奈良時代]]の[[貴族]]。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[朝臣]]。[[官位]]は[[正五位|正五位下]]・[[按察使]]。
== 経歴 == [[元明天皇|元明朝]]の[[和銅]]元年（[[708年]]）[[従六位|従六位上]]から三階昇進して[[従五位|従五位下]]に[[叙爵]]する。元明朝末の和銅7年（[[714年]]）3月に従五位上に昇叙され、同年11月に[[新羅使]]に応対するために左右将軍の任命が行われた際、広人は右副将軍に任ぜられた。
[[元正天皇|元正朝]]では当初[[弁官|右少弁]]を務め、[[霊亀]]3年（[[717年]]）3月に当時[[太政官]]の首班にあった[[左大臣]]・[[石上麻呂]]の[[葬儀]]にあたって、太政官の誄を宣べた<ref>『続日本紀』養老元年3月3日条</ref>。同年4月に[[大和国#国司|大倭守]]に転じるとのち[[按察使]]に遷るなど元正朝半ばより地方官を歴任する。[[養老]]4年（[[720年]]）正月に[[正五位|正五位下]]に叙せられるが、同年9月に[[蝦夷]]の反乱により任地の[[陸奥国]]で殺害された。最終[[官位]]は按察使正五位下。
== 官歴 == 『[[続日本紀]]』による。 *時期不詳：[[従六位|従六位上]] *[[和銅]]元年（[[708年]]） 正月11日：[[従五位|従五位下]] *和銅7年（[[714年]]） 3月28日：従五位上。11月26日：副将軍 *[[霊亀]]3年（[[717年]]） 3月3日：見[[弁官|右少弁]]。4月26日：[[大和国#国司|大倭守]] *時期不詳：[[按察使]] *[[養老]]4年（[[720年]]） 正月11日：[[正五位|正五位下]]。9月：[[崩御#卒去|卒去]]（按察使正五位下）
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *[[宇治谷孟]]『続日本紀 （上）』[[講談社]]〈[[講談社学術文庫]]〉、1992年
{{DEFAULTSORT:かみつけの の ひろひと}} [[Category:奈良時代の貴族]] [[Category:上毛野氏|ひろひと]] [[Category:8世紀生]] [[Category:720年没]]

revision.text:
'''上毛野 広人'''（かみつけの の ひろひと、生年不詳 - [[養老]]4年（[[720年]]）9月）は、[[奈良時代]]の[[貴族]]。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[朝臣]]。[[官位]]は[[正五位|正五位下]]・[[按察使]]。
== 経歴 == [[元明天皇|元明朝]]の[[和銅]]元年（[[708年]]）[[従六位|従六位上]]から三階昇進して[[従五位|従五位下]]に[[叙爵]]する。元明朝末の和銅7年（[[714年]]）3月に従五位上に昇叙され、同年11月に[[新羅使]]に応対するために左右将軍の任命が行われた際、広人は右副将軍に任ぜられた。
[[元正天皇|元正朝]]では当初[[弁官|右少弁]]を務め、[[霊亀]]3年（[[717年]]）3月に当時[[太政官]]の首班にあった[[左大臣]]・[[石上麻呂]]の[[葬儀]]にあたって、太政官の[[誄]]を宣べた<ref>『続日本紀』養老元年3月3日条</ref>。同年4月に[[大和国#国司|大倭守]]に転じるとのち[[按察使]]に遷るなど元正朝半ばより地方官を歴任する。[[養老]]4年（[[720年]]）正月に[[正五位|正五位下]]に叙せられるが、同年9月に[[蝦夷]]の反乱により任地の[[陸奥国]]で殺害された。最終[[官位]]は按察使正五位下。
== 官歴 == 『[[続日本紀]]』による。 *時期不詳：[[従六位|従六位上]] *[[和銅]]元年（[[708年]]） 正月11日：[[従五位|従五位下]] *和銅7年（[[714年]]） 3月28日：従五位上。11月26日：副将軍 *[[霊亀]]3年（[[717年]]） 3月3日：見[[弁官|右少弁]]。4月26日：[[大和国#国司|大倭守]] *時期不詳：[[按察使]] *[[養老]]4年（[[720年]]） 正月11日：[[正五位|正五位下]]。9月：[[崩御#卒去|卒去]]（按察使正五位下）
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *[[宇治谷孟]]『続日本紀 （上）』[[講談社]]〈[[講談社学術文庫]]〉、1992年

[[元正天皇|元正朝]]では当初[[弁官|右少弁]]を務め、[[霊亀]]3年（[[717年]]）3月に当時[[太政官]]の首班にあった[[左大臣]]・[[石上麻呂]]の葬儀にあたって、太政官の[[誄]]を宣べた<ref>『続日本紀』養老元年3月3日条</ref>。同年4月に[[大和国#国司|大倭守]]に転じるとのち按察使に遷るなど元正朝半ばより地方官を歴任する。[[養老]]4年（[[720年]]）正月に正五位下に叙せられるが、同年9月に[[蝦夷]]の反乱により任地の[[陸奥国]]で殺害された<ref>『続日本紀』養老4年9月28日条</ref>。最終官位は按察使正五位下。
=== 殺害後 === 史上初の大規模な蝦夷による反に対し、当時の朝廷は権力者であった[[藤原不比等]]が8月に没して[[長屋王]]が政権を握ったばかりであったが、乱の報を受け迅速に対応した。朝廷に報が届いた翌日の9月29日には早くも、[[遣唐使]]使節団を率いた統率力と東国の地方官（武蔵国守、相模国・上野国・下野国按察使）を務めた経験を買われた[[多治比縣守]]が[[征夷大将軍|持節征夷将軍]]に任じられ二度目の節刀を授けられ、縣守は副将軍[[下毛野石代]]、[[鎮狄将軍|持節鎮狄将軍]]・[[阿倍駿河]]と共に反乱鎮圧のために軍を率いて[[東北地方]]へ遠征した。
[[宮城県]][[大崎市]]にある[[官衙]]遺跡の権現山遺跡・三輪田遺跡と南小林遺跡が火災によって廃絶していることが近代の発掘調査から明らかとなっており、反乱はこの大崎地方で起こり、その際に遺跡の焼失が発生したものと考えられている。反乱鎮圧は早々にある程度の戦果を挙げたらしく、養老5年（[[721年]]）正月に多治比縣守は正四位上に昇叙され、4月には安倍駿河らと共に[[平城京]]へ帰還した。
== 官歴 == 『[[続日本紀]]』による。 *時期不詳：[[従六位|従六位上]] *[[和銅]]元年（[[708年]]） 正月11日：[[従五位|従五位下]] *和銅7年（[[714年]]） 3月28日：従五位上。11月26日：副将軍 *[[霊亀]]3年（[[717年]]） 3月3日：見[[弁官|右少弁]]。4月26日：[[大和国#国司|大倭守]] *時期不詳：[[按察使]] *[[養老]]4年（[[720年]]） 正月11日：[[正五位|正五位下]]。9月：卒去（按察使正五位下）
== 関連作品 == <!--[[Wikipedia:関連作品]]より「記事の対象が、大きな役割を担っている（主役、準主役、メインキャラクター、キーパーソン、メインレギュラー、メインライバル、メイン敵役、ラスボス等）わけではない作品」や未作成記事作品を追加しないで下さい。--> *[[山浦玄嗣]]『ホルケウ英雄伝 この国のいと小さき者』上下(KADOKAWA、2016/12、ISBN 9784041048283, ISBN 9784041050163)<!--ラスボス-->
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *[[宇治谷孟]]『続日本紀 （上）』[[講談社]]〈[[講談社学術文庫]]〉、1992年

revision.text:
'''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and administrator during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} and attained the rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下}}. He continued to rise in rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}, Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}}. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Ishikawa no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}, the {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}} as {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==
* {{Cite book|author=Ujitani Takeshi|title=Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)|publisher=Kodansha|series=Kodansha Academic Library|year=1992|isbn=9784061583487}}
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Inspector of Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥按察使}}

'''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and administrator during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} and attained the rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使}}.

Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下}}. He continued to rise in rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}, Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}}. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Ishikawa no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}, the {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.

In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}} as {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province

revision.text:
'''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and administrator during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} and attained the rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下}}. He continued to rise in rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}, Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}}. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Ishikawa no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}, the {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}} as {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}

revision.text:
'''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and administrator during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} and attained the rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下}}. He continued to rise in rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}, Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}}. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Ishikawa no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}, the {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}} as {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==
* {{Cite book|author=Ujitani Takeshi|title=Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)|publisher=Kodansha|series=Kodansha Academic Library|year=1992|isbn=9784061583487}}
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Inspector of Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥按察使}}

According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province

Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.

revision.text:
'''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and administrator during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} and attained the rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下}}. He continued to rise in rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}, Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}}. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Ishikawa no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}, the {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}} as {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==
* {{Cite book|author=Ujitani Takeshi|title=Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)|publisher=Kodansha|series=Kodansha Academic Library|year=1992|isbn=9784061583487}}
== See Also ==

In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Ishikawa no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}, the {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.

revision.text:
'''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and administrator during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} and attained the rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下}}. He continued to rise in rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}, Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}}. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Ishikawa no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}, the {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}} as {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==
* {{Cite book|author=Ujitani Takeshi|title=Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)|publisher=Kodansha|series=Kodansha Academic Library|year=1992|isbn=9784061583487}}
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Inspector of Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥按察使}}

In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>

This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>

According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province

Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.

revision.text:
'''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and administrator during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} and attained the rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下}}. He continued to rise in rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}, Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}}. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Ishikawa no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}, the {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}} as {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and administrator during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} and attained the rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下}}. He continued to rise in rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}, Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}}. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Ishikawa no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}, the {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}} as {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==
* {{Cite book|author=Ujitani Takeshi|title=Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)|publisher=Kodansha|series=Kodansha Academic Library|year=1992|isbn=9784061583487}}
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Inspector of Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥按察使}}

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Pages needing attribution]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and administrator during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} and attained the rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下}}. He continued to rise in rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}, Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}}. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Ishikawa no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}, the {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}} as {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==
* {{Cite book|author=Ujitani Takeshi|title=Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)|publisher=Kodansha|series=Kodansha Academic Library|year=1992|isbn=9784061583487}}
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Inspector of Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥按察使}}

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and administrator during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} and attained the rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下}}. He continued to rise in rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}, Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}}. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Ishikawa no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}, the {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}} as {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==
* {{Cite book|author=Ujitani Takeshi|title=Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)|publisher=Kodansha|series=Kodansha Academic Library|year=1992|isbn=9784061583487}}
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Inspector of Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥按察使}}

'''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and administrator during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} and attained the rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下}}. He continued to rise in rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}, Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}}. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Ishikawa no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}, the {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}} as {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==
* {{Cite book|author=Ujitani Takeshi|title=Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)|publisher=Kodansha|series=Kodansha Academic Library|year=1992|isbn=9784061583487}}
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Inspector of Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥按察使}}

revision.comment: NAMESPACE
revision.text:
'''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and administrator during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} and attained the rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下}}. He continued to rise in rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}, Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}}. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Ishikawa no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}, the {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}} as {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==
* {{Cite book|author=Ujitani Takeshi|title=Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)|publisher=Kodansha|series=Kodansha Academic Library|year=1992|isbn=9784061583487}}
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Inspector of Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥按察使}}

revision.text:
'''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and administrator during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} and attained the rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下}}. He continued to rise in rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}, Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}}. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Ishikawa no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}, the {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}} as {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==
* {{Cite book|author=Ujitani Takeshi|title=Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)|publisher=Kodansha|series=Kodansha Academic Library|year=1992|isbn=9784061583487}}
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Inspector of Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥按察使}}

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and administrator during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} and attained the rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下}}. He continued to rise in rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}, Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}}. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Ishikawa no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}, the {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}} as {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==
* {{Cite book|author=Ujitani Takeshi|title=Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)|publisher=Kodansha|series=Kodansha Academic Library|year=1992|isbn=9784061583487}}
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Inspector of Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥按察使}}

revision.comment: Removing this excess
revision.text:
'''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and administrator during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} and attained the rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下}}. He continued to rise in rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}, Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}}. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Ishikawa no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}, the {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}} as {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==
* {{Cite book|author=Ujitani Takeshi|title=Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)|publisher=Kodansha|series=Kodansha Academic Library|year=1992|isbn=9784061583487}}
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Inspector of Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥按察使}}

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and administrator during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} and attained the rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下}}. He continued to rise in rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}, Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}}. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Ishikawa no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}, the {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}} as {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==
* {{Cite book|author=Ujitani Takeshi|title=Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)|publisher=Kodansha|series=Kodansha Academic Library|year=1992|isbn=9784061583487}}
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Inspector of Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥按察使}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "-qq]]" to "-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]]"
revision.text:
'''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and administrator during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} and attained the rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下}}. He continued to rise in rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}, Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}}. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Ishikawa no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}, the {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}} as {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==
* {{Cite book|author=Ujitani Takeshi|title=Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)|publisher=Kodansha|series=Kodansha Academic Library|year=1992|isbn=9784061583487}}
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Inspector of Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥按察使}}

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and administrator during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} and attained the rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下}}. He continued to rise in rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}, Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}}. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Ishikawa no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}, the {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}} as {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==
* {{Cite book|author=Ujitani Takeshi|title=Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)|publisher=Kodansha|series=Kodansha Academic Library|year=1992|isbn=9784061583487}}
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Inspector of Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥按察使}}

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and administrator during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} and attained the rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下}}. He continued to rise in rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}, Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}}. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Ishikawa no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}, the {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}} as {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==
* {{Cite book|author=Ujitani Takeshi|title=Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)|publisher=Kodansha|series=Kodansha Academic Library|year=1992|isbn=9784061583487}}
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Inspector of Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥按察使}}

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and administrator during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} and attained the rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下}}. He continued to rise in rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}, Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}}. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Ishikawa no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}, the {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}} as {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==
* {{Cite book|author=Ujitani Takeshi|title=Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)|publisher=Kodansha|series=Kodansha Academic Library|year=1992|isbn=9784061583487}}
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Inspector of Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥按察使}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|従五位下}}" to "|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}"
revision.text:
'''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and administrator during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} and attained the rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. He continued to rise in rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}, Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}}. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Ishikawa no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}, the {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}} as {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==
* {{Cite book|author=Ujitani Takeshi|title=Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)|publisher=Kodansha|series=Kodansha Academic Library|year=1992|isbn=9784061583487}}
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Inspector of Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥按察使}}

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Qq deprecated open]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and administrator during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} and attained the rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. He continued to rise in rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}, Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}}. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Ishikawa no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}, the {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}} as {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==
* {{Cite book|author=Ujitani Takeshi|title=Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)|publisher=Kodansha|series=Kodansha Academic Library|year=1992|isbn=9784061583487}}
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Inspector of Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥按察使}}
