'''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and administrator during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} and attained the rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. He continued to rise in rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, qq/26</ref>
During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}, Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}}. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Ishikawa no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}, the {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}} as {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (qq/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (1/qq); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==
* {{Cite book|author=Ujitani Takeshi|title=Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)|publisher=Kodansha|series=Kodansha Academic Library|year=1992|isbn=9784061583487}}
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Inspector of Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥按察使}}

This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (qq/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (1/qq); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province

Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.

revision.text:
'''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and administrator during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} and attained the rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. He continued to rise in rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, qq/26</ref>
During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}, Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}}. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Ishikawa no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}, the {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}} as {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (qq/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (1/qq); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==
* {{Cite book|author=Ujitani Takeshi|title=Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)|publisher=Kodansha|series=Kodansha Academic Library|year=1992|isbn=9784061583487}}
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Inspector of Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥按察使}}

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and administrator during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} and attained the rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. He continued to rise in rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, qq/26</ref>
During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}, Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}}. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Ishikawa no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}, the {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}} as {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (qq/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (1/qq); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==
* {{Cite book|author=Ujitani Takeshi|title=Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)|publisher=Kodansha|series=Kodansha Academic Library|year=1992|isbn=9784061583487}}
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Inspector of Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥按察使}}

revision.text:
'''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and administrator during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} and attained the rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. He continued to rise in rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, qq/26</ref>
During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}, Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}}. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Ishikawa no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}, the {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}} as {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (qq/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (1/qq); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==
* {{Cite book|author=Ujitani Takeshi|title=Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)|publisher=Kodansha|series=Kodansha Academic Library|year=1992|isbn=9784061583487}}
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Inspector of Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥按察使}}

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and administrator during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} and attained the rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. He continued to rise in rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, qq/26</ref>
During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}, Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}}. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Ishikawa no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}, the {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}} as {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (qq/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (1/qq); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==
* {{Cite book|author=Ujitani Takeshi|title=Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)|publisher=Kodansha|series=Kodansha Academic Library|year=1992|isbn=9784061583487}}
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Inspector of Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥按察使}}

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Ngoodbye $1]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and administrator during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} and attained the rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. He continued to rise in rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, qq/26</ref>
During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}, Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}}. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Ishikawa no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}, the {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}} as {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (qq/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (1/qq); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==
* {{Cite book|author=Ujitani Takeshi|title=Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)|publisher=Kodansha|series=Kodansha Academic Library|year=1992|isbn=9784061583487}}
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Inspector of Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥按察使}}

revision.text:
'''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and administrator during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} and attained the rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. He continued to rise in rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, qq/26</ref>
During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}, Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}}. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Ishikawa no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}, the {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}} as {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (qq/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (1/qq); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==
* {{Cite book|author=Ujitani Takeshi|title=Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)|publisher=Kodansha|series=Kodansha Academic Library|year=1992|isbn=9784061583487}}
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Inspector of Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥按察使}}

He continued to rise in rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>

In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}. Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.

In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}} as {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.

'''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and administrator during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} and attained the rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. He continued to rise in rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}, Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}}. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Ishikawa no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}, the {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}} as {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}

According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.

revision.text:
'''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and administrator during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} and attained the rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. He continued to rise in rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}, Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}}. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Ishikawa no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}, the {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}} as {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==

== See Also ==

revision.text:
'''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and administrator during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} and attained the rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. He continued to rise in rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}, Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}}. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Ishikawa no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}, the {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}} as {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}

According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵↵== See Also ==" to "== See Also =="
revision.text:
'''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and administrator during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} and attained the rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. He continued to rise in rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}, Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}}. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Ishikawa no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}, the {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}} as {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==

Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also ==" to " == See Also! =="
revision.text:
'''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and administrator during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} and attained the rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. He continued to rise in rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}, Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}}. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Ishikawa no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}, the {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}} as {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}
== References ==

== Bibliography ==

* {{ill|Inspector of Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥按察使}}

According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==

revision.comment: Text replacement - "3年" to "3rd year "
revision.text:
'''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and administrator during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} and attained the rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. He continued to rise in rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}, Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}}. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3rd year }} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Ishikawa no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}, the {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}} as {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3rd year }} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}
== References ==

== Bibliography ==

Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "7年" to "7th year "
revision.text:
'''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and administrator during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} and attained the rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. He continued to rise in rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7th year }} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}, Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}}. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3rd year }} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Ishikawa no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}, the {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4th year }} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}} as {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7th year }} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3rd year }} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4th year }} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==

According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7th year }} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3rd year }} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4th year }} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.

'''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and administrator during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} and attained the rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. He continued to rise in rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7th year }} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}, Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}}. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3rd year }} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Ishikawa no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}, the {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4th year }} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}} as {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7th year }} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3rd year }} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4th year }} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}

revision.text:
'''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and administrator during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣}} and attained the rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇}}. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. He continued to rise in rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7th year }} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}}, Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官}}. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3rd year }} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Ishikawa no Maro|ja|石上麻呂}}, the {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4th year }} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}} as {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7th year }} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3rd year }} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4th year }} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==
* {{Cite book|author=Ujitani Takeshi|title=Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)|publisher=Kodansha|series=Kodansha Academic Library|year=1992|isbn=9784061583487}} == See Also ==
* {{ill|Inspector of Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥按察使}}

=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4th year }} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}} as {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7th year }} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3rd year }} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4th year }} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}
== References ==

== Bibliography ==
* {{Cite book|author=Ujitani Takeshi|title=Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)|publisher=Kodansha|series=Kodansha Academic Library|year=1992|isbn=9784061583487}} == See Also ==

revision.text:
'''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族|12=draft}} and administrator during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft}}. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|12=draft}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣|12=draft}} and attained the rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=draft}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使|12=draft}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇|12=draft}}. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年|12=draft}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|12=draft}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=draft}}. He continued to rise in rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7th year|12=draft}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅|12=draft}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇|12=draft}}, Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官|12=draft}}. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3rd year|12=draft}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Ishikawa no Maro|ja|石上麻呂|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣|12=draft}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守|12=draft}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4th year|12=draft}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=draft}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province|12=draft}}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|12=draft}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|12=draft}} as {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍|12=draft}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市|12=draft}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県|12=draft}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|12=draft}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年|12=draft}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=draft}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7th year|12=draft}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3rd year|12=draft}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁|12=draft}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守|12=draft}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4th year|12=draft}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=draft}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.

In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3rd year|12=draft|12=draft}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Ishikawa no Maro|ja|石上麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守|12=draft|12=draft}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=draft|12=draft}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province|12=draft|12=draft}}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|12=draft|12=draft}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|12=draft|12=draft}} as {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍|12=draft|12=draft}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市|12=draft|12=draft}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>

According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|12=draft|12=draft}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年|12=draft|12=draft}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft|12=draft}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3rd year|12=draft|12=draft}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁|12=draft|12=draft}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守|12=draft|12=draft}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=draft|12=draft}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
