Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Genmei|qq=}}. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 1|qq=}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūroku-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}. He continued to rise in Rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 7|qq=}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Silla|qq=}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Genshō|qq=}}, Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Minor Controller|qq=}} in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官|1=Daijō-kan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-kan|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-kan|qq=}}. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|qq=}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Ishikawa no Maro|ja|石上麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Maro|qq=}}, the {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣|1=Minister of the Left|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadaijin|qq=}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Yamato|qq=}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|qq=}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province|2=ja|3=陸奥国|ja|Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province|2=ja|3=陸奥国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsu Province|qq=}}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi|qq=}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Agatamori|qq=}} as {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seii Taishōgun|qq=}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaki|qq=}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|10=ja|11=宮城県|ja|宮城県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture|qq=}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoku Nihongi|qq=}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūroku-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 1|qq=}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 7|qq=}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|qq=}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Minor Controller|qq=}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Yamato|qq=}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|qq=}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==

In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|qq=}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province|2=ja|3=陸奥国|ja|Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province|2=ja|3=陸奥国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsu Province|qq=}}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi|qq=}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Agatamori|qq=}} as {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seii Taishōgun|qq=}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaki|qq=}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|10=ja|11=宮城県|ja|宮城県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture|qq=}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoku Nihongi|qq=}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūroku-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 1|qq=}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 7|qq=}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|qq=}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Minor Controller|qq=}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Yamato|qq=}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|qq=}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.

revision.text:
'''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/noble|qq=}} and administrator during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara period|qq=}}. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|qq=}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ason|qq=}} and attained the Rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inspector (Ancient Japan)|qq=}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Genmei|qq=}}. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 1|qq=}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūroku-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}. He continued to rise in Rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 7|qq=}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Silla|qq=}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Genshō|qq=}}, Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Minor Controller|qq=}} in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官|1=Daijō-kan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-kan|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-kan|qq=}}. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|qq=}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Ishikawa no Maro|ja|石上麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Maro|qq=}}, the {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣|1=Minister of the Left|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadaijin|qq=}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Yamato|qq=}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|qq=}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province|2=ja|3=陸奥国|ja|Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province|2=ja|3=陸奥国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsu Province|qq=}}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi|qq=}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Agatamori|qq=}} as {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seii Taishōgun|qq=}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaki|qq=}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|10=ja|11=宮城県|ja|宮城県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture|qq=}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoku Nihongi|qq=}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūroku-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 1|qq=}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 7|qq=}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|qq=}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Minor Controller|qq=}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Yamato|qq=}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|qq=}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamauchi Genji|qq=}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|qq=}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "rd year|" to "年|"
revision.text:
'''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/noble|qq=}} and administrator during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara period|qq=}}. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|qq=}} of {{ill|Ason|ja|朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ason|qq=}} and attained the Rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inspector (Ancient Japan)|qq=}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Genmei|qq=}}. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 1|qq=}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūroku-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}. He continued to rise in Rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 7|qq=}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Silla|qq=}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Genshō|qq=}}, Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Minor Controller|qq=}} in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|ja|太政官|1=Daijō-kan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-kan|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijō-kan|qq=}}. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|qq=}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Ishikawa no Maro|ja|石上麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Maro|qq=}}, the {{ill|Sadaijin|ja|左大臣|1=Minister of the Left|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadaijin|qq=}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Yamato|qq=}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|qq=}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province|2=ja|3=陸奥国|ja|Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province|2=ja|3=陸奥国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsu Province|qq=}}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi|qq=}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Agatamori|qq=}} as {{ill|Seii Taishōgun|ja|征夷大将軍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seii Taishōgun|qq=}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki|ja|大崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōsaki|qq=}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|10=ja|11=宮城県|ja|宮城県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagi Prefecture|qq=}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoku Nihongi|qq=}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūroku-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 1|qq=}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 7|qq=}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|qq=}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Minor Controller|qq=}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Yamato|qq=}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|qq=}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamauchi Genji|qq=}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|qq=}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==
* {{Cite book|author=Ujitani Takeshi|title=Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)|publisher=Kodansha|series=Kodansha Academic Library|year=1992|isbn=9784061583487}}
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Inspector of Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥按察使|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inspector of Mutsu Province|qq=}}

In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|qq=}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province|2=ja|3=陸奥国|ja|Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province|2=ja|3=陸奥国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsu Province|qq=}}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]], marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Agatamori|qq=}} as [[:en:Shogun|Seii Taishōgun]] to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the [[:en:Ōsaki, Miyagi|Ōsaki]] region of present-day [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]].<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūroku-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 1|qq=}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 7|qq=}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|qq=}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Minor Controller|qq=}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Yamato|qq=}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|qq=}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamauchi Genji|qq=}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|qq=}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}

[[sn:Kamitsukeno no Hirohito]] [[ja:上毛野広人]] [[sn:amitsukeno no Hirohito]] [[en:Kamitsukeno no Hirohito]]
<!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--> '''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a [[:en:Nobility|noble]] and administrator during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|qq=}} of [[:en:Ason|Ason]] and attained the Rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inspector (Ancient Japan)|qq=}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]]. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 1|qq=}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūroku-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}. He continued to rise in Rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 7|qq=}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from [[:en:Silla|Silla]].<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]], Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Minor Controller|qq=}} in the [[:en:Daijō-kan|Daijō-kan]]. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|qq=}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Ishikawa no Maro]], the [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]].<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Yamato|qq=}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|qq=}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province|2=ja|3=陸奥国|ja|Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province|2=ja|3=陸奥国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsu Province|qq=}}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]], marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Agatamori|qq=}} as [[:en:Shogun|Seii Taishōgun]] to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the [[:en:Ōsaki, Miyagi|Ōsaki]] region of present-day [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]].<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūroku-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 1|qq=}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 7|qq=}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|qq=}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Minor Controller|qq=}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Yamato|qq=}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|qq=}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province

In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|qq=}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Ishikawa no Maro]], the [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]].<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Yamato|qq=}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|qq=}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province|2=ja|3=陸奥国|ja|Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province|2=ja|3=陸奥国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsu Province|qq=}}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]], marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Agatamori|qq=}} as [[:en:Shogun|Seii Taishōgun]] to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the [[:en:Ōsaki, Miyagi|Ōsaki]] region of present-day [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]].<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūroku-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 1|qq=}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 7|qq=}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|qq=}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Minor Controller|qq=}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Yamato|qq=}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|qq=}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.

He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|qq=}} of [[:en:Ason|Ason]] and attained the Rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inspector (Ancient Japan)|qq=}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]]. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 1|qq=}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūroku-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}. He continued to rise in Rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 7|qq=}} (714). Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.

== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]]. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 1|qq=}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūroku-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}. He continued to rise in Rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 7|qq=}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from [[:en:Silla|Silla]].<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]], Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Minor Controller|qq=}} in the [[:en:Daijō-kan|Daijō-kan]]. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|qq=}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Ishikawa no Maro]], the [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]].<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Yamato|qq=}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|qq=}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province|2=ja|3=陸奥国|ja|Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province|2=ja|3=陸奥国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsu Province|qq=}}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]], marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Agatamori|qq=}} as [[:en:Shogun|Seii Taishōgun]] to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the [[:en:Ōsaki, Miyagi|Ōsaki]] region of present-day [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]].<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūroku-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 1|qq=}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 7|qq=}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|qq=}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Minor Controller|qq=}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Yamato|qq=}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|qq=}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamauchi Genji|qq=}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|qq=}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}

[[sn:Kamitsukeno no Hirohito]] [[ja:上毛野広人]] [[sn:amitsukeno no Hirohito]] [[en:Kamitsukeno no Hirohito]]
<!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--> '''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a [[:en:Nobility|noble]] and administrator during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|qq=}} of [[:en:Ason|Ason]] and attained the Rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inspector (Ancient Japan)|qq=}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]]. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 1|qq=}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūroku-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}. He continued to rise in Rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 7|qq=}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from [[:en:Silla|Silla]].<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]], Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Minor Controller|qq=}} in the [[:en:Daijō-kan|Daijō-kan]]. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|qq=}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Ishikawa no Maro]], the [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]].<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Yamato|qq=}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|qq=}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province|2=ja|3=陸奥国|ja|Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province|2=ja|3=陸奥国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsu Province|qq=}}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]], marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Agatamori|qq=}} as [[:en:Shogun|Seii Taishōgun]] to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the [[:en:Ōsaki, Miyagi|Ōsaki]] region of present-day [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]].<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūroku-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 1|qq=}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 7|qq=}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|qq=}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Minor Controller|qq=}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Yamato|qq=}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|qq=}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamauchi Genji|qq=}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|qq=}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}

revision.comment: Bot: remove invalid enwiki interwiki links
revision.text:
'''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a [[:en:Nobility|noble]] and administrator during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|qq=}} of [[:en:Ason|Ason]] and attained the Rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inspector (Ancient Japan)|qq=}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]]. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 1|qq=}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūroku-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}. He continued to rise in Rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 7|qq=}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from [[:en:Silla|Silla]].<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]], Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Minor Controller|qq=}} in the [[:en:Daijō-kan|Daijō-kan]]. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|qq=}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Ishikawa no Maro]], the [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]].<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Yamato|qq=}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|qq=}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province|2=ja|3=陸奥国|ja|Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province|2=ja|3=陸奥国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsu Province|qq=}}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]], marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Agatamori|qq=}} as [[:en:Shogun|Seii Taishōgun]] to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the [[:en:Ōsaki, Miyagi|Ōsaki]] region of present-day [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]].<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūroku-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 1|qq=}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 7|qq=}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|qq=}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Minor Controller|qq=}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Yamato|qq=}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|qq=}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamauchi Genji|qq=}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|qq=}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography ==
* {{Cite book|author=Ujitani Takeshi|title=Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)|publisher=Kodansha|series=Kodansha Academic Library|year=1992|isbn=9784061583487}}
== See Also ==

{{translated page|ja|上毛野広人|version=99541943|comment=Imported full ja history}}

According to the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūroku-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従六位}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 1|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和銅}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 7|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Minor Controller|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Yamato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.

[[sn:Kamitsukeno no Hirohito]] [[ja:上毛野広人]] [[sn:amitsukeno no Hirohito]]
<!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--> '''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a [[:en:Nobility|noble]] and administrator during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of [[:en:Ason|Ason]] and attained the Rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inspector (Ancient Japan)|qq=}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]]. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 1|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和銅}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūroku-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. He continued to rise in Rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 7|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from [[:en:Silla|Silla]].<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]], Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Minor Controller|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官}} in the [[:en:Daijō-kan|Daijō-kan]]. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Ishikawa no Maro]], the [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]].<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Yamato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} and assigned as Inspector of [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]], marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Agatamori|qq=}} as [[:en:Shogun|Seii Taishōgun]] to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the [[:en:Ōsaki, Miyagi|Ōsaki]] region of present-day [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]].<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūroku-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従六位}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 1|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和銅}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 7|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Minor Controller|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Yamato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.

He continued to rise in Rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 7|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from [[:en:Silla|Silla]].<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]], Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Minor Controller|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官}} in the [[:en:Daijō-kan|Daijō-kan]]. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Ishikawa no Maro]], the [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]].<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Yamato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} and assigned as Inspector of [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]], marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Agatamori|qq=}} as [[:en:Shogun|Seii Taishōgun]] to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the [[:en:Ōsaki, Miyagi|Ōsaki]] region of present-day [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]].<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūroku-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従六位}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 1|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和銅}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 7|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Minor Controller|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Yamato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.

'''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a [[:en:Nobility|noble]] and administrator during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of [[:en:Ason|Ason]] and attained the Rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inspector (Ancient Japan)|qq=}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]]. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 1|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和銅}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūroku-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. He continued to rise in Rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 7|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from [[:en:Silla|Silla]].<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]], Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Minor Controller|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官}} in the [[:en:Daijō-kan|Daijō-kan]]. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Ishikawa no Maro]], the [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]].<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Yamato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} and assigned as Inspector of [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]], marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Agatamori|qq=}} as [[:en:Shogun|Seii Taishōgun]] to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the [[:en:Ōsaki, Miyagi|Ōsaki]] region of present-day [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]].<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūroku-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従六位}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 1|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和銅}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 7|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Minor Controller|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Yamato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province

Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Nara period nobles
revision.text:
'''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a [[:en:Nobility|noble]] and administrator during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of [[:en:Ason|Ason]] and attained the Rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inspector (Ancient Japan)|qq=}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of [[:en:Empress Genmei|Empress Genmei]]. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 1|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和銅}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūroku-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. He continued to rise in Rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 7|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from [[:en:Silla|Silla]].<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]], Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Minor Controller|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官}} in the [[:en:Daijō-kan|Daijō-kan]]. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of [[:en:Isonokami no Maro|Ishikawa no Maro]], the [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]].<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Yamato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} and assigned as Inspector of [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]], marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Agatamori|qq=}} as [[:en:Shogun|Seii Taishōgun]] to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the [[:en:Ōsaki, Miyagi|Ōsaki]] region of present-day [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]].<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūroku-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従六位}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 1|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和銅}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 7|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Minor Controller|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Yamato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamauchi Genji|qq=}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}
