<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上毛野広人]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上毛野広人]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上毛野広人]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:上毛野広人]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0750|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}}}
'''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a {{ill|Nobility|en|Nobility|WD=Q134737|lt=noble|1=Nobility}} and administrator during the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of {{ill|Ason|en|Ason|WD=Q4807592|lt=Ason|1=Ason}} and attained the Rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inspector (Ancient Japan)|1=Provincial Judge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q848663}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|en|Empress Genmei|WD=Q233224|lt=Empress Genmei|1=Empress Genmei}}. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 1|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和銅|1=Wadō (era)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1373026}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūroku-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. He continued to rise in Rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla|1=Silla}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|en|Empress Genshō|WD=Q235343|lt=Empress Genshō|1=Empress Genshō}}, Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Minor Controller|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官|1=benkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270768}} in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|en|Daijō-kan|WD=Q1192939|lt=Daijō-kan|1=Daijō-kan}}. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Isonokami no Maro|en|Isonokami no Maro|WD=Q6086140|lt=Ishikawa no Maro|1=Isonokami no Maro}}, the {{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|WD=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left|1=Minister of the Left}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Yamato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|en|Mutsu Province|WD=Q907389|lt=Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province}}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|WD=Q932574|lt=Emishi|1=Emishi}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Agatamori|qq=}} as {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Seii Taishōgun|1=Shogun}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|en|Ōsaki, Miyagi|WD=Q385892|lt=Ōsaki|1=Ōsaki, Miyagi}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihongi}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūroku-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従六位}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 1|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和銅|1=Wadō (era)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1373026}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Minor Controller|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官|1=benkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270768}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Yamato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamauchi Genji|1=Harutsugu Yamaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469999}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}

In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Agatamori|qq=}} as {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Seii Taishōgun|1=Shogun}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|en|Ōsaki, Miyagi|WD=Q385892|lt=Ōsaki|1=Ōsaki, Miyagi}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihongi}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūroku-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従六位}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 1|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和銅|1=Wadō (era)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1373026}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Minor Controller|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官|1=benkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270768}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Yamato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.

<!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--> '''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a {{ill|Nobility|en|Nobility|WD=Q134737|lt=noble|1=Nobility}} and administrator during the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of {{ill|Ason|en|Ason|WD=Q4807592|lt=Ason|1=Ason}} and attained the Rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inspector (Ancient Japan)|1=Provincial Judge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q848663}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|en|Empress Genmei|WD=Q233224|lt=Empress Genmei|1=Empress Genmei}}. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 1|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和銅|1=Wadō (era)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1373026}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūroku-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. He continued to rise in Rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla|1=Silla}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|en|Empress Genshō|WD=Q235343|lt=Empress Genshō|1=Empress Genshō}}, Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Minor Controller|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官|1=benkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270768}} in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|en|Daijō-kan|WD=Q1192939|lt=Daijō-kan|1=Daijō-kan}}. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Isonokami no Maro|en|Isonokami no Maro|WD=Q6086140|lt=Ishikawa no Maro|1=Isonokami no Maro}}, the {{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|WD=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left|1=Minister of the Left}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Yamato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|en|Mutsu Province|WD=Q907389|lt=Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province}}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|WD=Q932574|lt=Emishi|1=Emishi}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Agatamori|qq=}} as {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Seii Taishōgun|1=Shogun}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|en|Ōsaki, Miyagi|WD=Q385892|lt=Ōsaki|1=Ōsaki, Miyagi}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==

[[ja:上毛野広人]]
<!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--><!--Kamitsukeno no Hirohito--> '''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a {{ill|Nobility|en|Nobility|WD=Q134737|lt=noble|1=Nobility}} and administrator during the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of {{ill|Ason|en|Ason|WD=Q4807592|lt=Ason|1=Ason}} and attained the Rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inspector (Ancient Japan)|1=Provincial Judge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q848663}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|en|Empress Genmei|WD=Q233224|lt=Empress Genmei|1=Empress Genmei}}. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 1|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和銅|1=Wadō (era)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1373026}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūroku-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. He continued to rise in Rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla|1=Silla}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|en|Empress Genshō|WD=Q235343|lt=Empress Genshō|1=Empress Genshō}}, Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Minor Controller|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官|1=benkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270768}} in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|en|Daijō-kan|WD=Q1192939|lt=Daijō-kan|1=Daijō-kan}}. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Isonokami no Maro|en|Isonokami no Maro|WD=Q6086140|lt=Ishikawa no Maro|1=Isonokami no Maro}}, the {{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|WD=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left|1=Minister of the Left}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Yamato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|en|Mutsu Province|WD=Q907389|lt=Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province}}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|WD=Q932574|lt=Emishi|1=Emishi}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Agatamori|qq=}} as {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Seii Taishōgun|1=Shogun}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|en|Ōsaki, Miyagi|WD=Q385892|lt=Ōsaki|1=Ōsaki, Miyagi}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihongi}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūroku-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従六位}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 1|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和銅|1=Wadō (era)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1373026}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Minor Controller|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官|1=benkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270768}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Yamato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==

Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.

This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|en|Ōsaki, Miyagi|qid=Q385892|lt=Ōsaki|1=Ōsaki, Miyagi}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|qid=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|qid=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihongi}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūroku-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従六位}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 1|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和銅|1=Wadō (era)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1373026}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Minor Controller|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官|1=benkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3270768}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Yamato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province

Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.

In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Agatamori|qq=}} as {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|qid=Q131767|lt=Seii Taishōgun|1=Shogun}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|en|Ōsaki, Miyagi|qid=Q385892|lt=Ōsaki|1=Ōsaki, Miyagi}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|qid=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|qid=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihongi}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūroku-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従六位}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 1|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和銅|1=Wadō (era)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1373026}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Minor Controller|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官|1=benkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3270768}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Yamato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.

revision.text:
{{Infobox person|birth_date=0750|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}}}
'''Kamitsukeno no Hirohito''' (上毛野広人) was a {{ill|Nobility|en|Nobility|qid=Q134737|lt=noble|1=Nobility}} and administrator during the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}. He held the {{ill|Kabane|ja|姓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of {{ill|Ason|en|Ason|qid=Q4807592|lt=Ason|1=Ason}} and attained the Rank of {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} while serving as an {{ill|Inspector (Ancient Japan)|ja|按察使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inspector (Ancient Japan)|1=Provincial Judge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q848663}}.
== Biography ==
Hirohito first appears in historical records during the reign of {{ill|Empress Genmei|en|Empress Genmei|qid=Q233224|lt=Empress Genmei|1=Empress Genmei}}. In {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 1|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和銅|1=Wadō (era)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1373026}} (708), he was promoted from {{ill|Jūroku-i|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūroku-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. He continued to rise in Rank, becoming {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} in {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (714). During this time, Hirohito was appointed as Vice General (右副将軍) to handle the diplomatic mission of an envoy from {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|qid=Q28456|lt=Silla|1=Silla}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Wadō 7, 11/26</ref>
During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|en|Empress Genshō|qid=Q235343|lt=Empress Genshō|1=Empress Genshō}}, Hirohito served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Minor Controller|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官|1=benkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3270768}} in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|en|Daijō-kan|qid=Q1192939|lt=Daijō-kan|1=Daijō-kan}}. In {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (717), he gave a eulogy on behalf of the government at the funeral of {{ill|Isonokami no Maro|en|Isonokami no Maro|qid=Q6086140|lt=Ishikawa no Maro|1=Isonokami no Maro}}, the {{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|qid=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left|1=Minister of the Left}}.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Reiki 3, 3/3</ref> Later that year, he was reassigned as {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Yamato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Hirohito was eventually appointed as an Inspector (按察使), responsible for overseeing regional governance.
=== Death and Rebellion ===
In {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (720), Hirohito was promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} and assigned as Inspector of {{ill|Mutsu Province|en|Mutsu Province|qid=Q907389|lt=Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province}}. Later that year, he was killed during a rebellion led by the {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|qid=Q932574|lt=Emishi|1=Emishi}}, marking one of the earliest large-scale uprisings against the central government.<ref>‘Shoku Nihongi,’ Yōrō 4, 9/28</ref>
In response to Hirohito’s death, the court swiftly appointed {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Agatamori|qq=}} as {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|qid=Q131767|lt=Seii Taishōgun|1=Shogun}} to suppress the rebellion. This conflict is linked to archaeological evidence of burned structures in the {{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|en|Ōsaki, Miyagi|qid=Q385892|lt=Ōsaki|1=Ōsaki, Miyagi}} region of present-day {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|qid=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}.<ref>Suzuki [2008: 45]</ref>
== Career Timeline ==
According to the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|qid=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihongi}}: * Date unknown: {{ill|Jūroku-i-jō|ja|従六位上|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūroku-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従六位}} * {{ill|Wadō 1|ja|和銅元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 1|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和銅|1=Wadō (era)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1373026}} (708): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} * {{ill|Wadō 7|ja|和銅7年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (714): Promoted to {{ill|Jūgo-i-jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgo-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (3/28); appointed Vice General (11/26) * {{ill|Reiki 3|ja|霊亀3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiki 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (717): Served as {{ill|Right Minor Controller|ja|右少弁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Right Minor Controller|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁官|1=benkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3270768}} (3/3); appointed {{ill|Governor of Yamato|ja|大倭守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Governor of Yamato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (4/26) * {{ill|Yōrō 4|ja|養老4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (720): Promoted to {{ill|Shōgo-i|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgo-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} (1/11); killed in September while serving as Inspector of Mutsu Province
== Legacy ==
Hirohito’s death highlighted the challenges faced by the central government in maintaining control over distant regions during the early Nara period. The rebellion he encountered underscores the complexities of integrating northern territories into the Yamato polity.
== Related Works ==
* {{ill|Yamauchi Genji|ja|山浦玄嗣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamauchi Genji|1=Harutsugu Yamaura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11469999}}, *{{ill|Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja|ホルケウ英雄伝 : この国のいと小さき者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horkeu Eiyūden: Kono Kuni no Ito Chiisaki Mono|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*, KADOKAWA, 2016. {{ISBN2|9784041048283}}
